title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish children in Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island.
Perry's training is inspired by the resilience and hope displayed by the Make-A-Wish children and their families.
Over 15 years, Perry has met many Wish children, who motivate him to continue fundraising and running marathons.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots expected to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million; $25 million guaranteed.
Mafe, 27, recorded 44 pressures and a 17.4% pass rush win rate last season with the Seahawks.
ESPN insider predicts Alec Pierce to sign with the Chargers, not Patriots, on a three-year, $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues on March 6, 2026, part of his "Under the Powerlines Tour."
The setlist included satirical songs on political and social issues, and covers of Black Sabbath and John Denver songs.
Welles' performance featured political commentary through music, with a focus on war and social media themes.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida, playing for the Red Sox, went 2-for-4, helping Japan lead pool play with a 2-0 record.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game at the Tokyo Dome, wearing a Dodgers uniform.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk is running her first Boston Marathon at age 25, representing Team Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk, a Roslindale native and Boston Public Schools teacher, supports Mass. Eye and Ear as a former patient.
Mass Eye and Ear is a research leader with the only specialized 24-hour emergency department for eye and ENT trauma in Massachusetts.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team in August 2025, joining Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler, ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst since 2000, will cover NBA Finals, marking a high profile role advancement.
ESPN/ABC's prior broadcast instability resolved with Legler's addition following departures of Doc Rivers and JJ Redick.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after 298 days, scoring 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists.
Maine native Cooper Flagg made his NBA debut at TD Garden, scoring 16 points for the Mavericks.
Flagg, a Celtics fan, received a warm reception from Boston fans despite playing for the visiting team.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Charles Barkley remains confident in the Cavaliers as favorites in the East despite Jayson Tatum's return to Celtics.
Jayson Tatum scores 15 points, with 12 rebounds and seven assists, in first game back after Achilles tear.
Boston Celtics have won 22 games since January 1, tying the most wins in the NBA with the Spurs.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers is accused of impersonating a U.S. Army veteran for over 30 years to obtain benefits.
Sommers allegedly stole the identity of a real veteran, amassing thousands in Social Security and VA benefits.
He faces up to five years in prison for making false statements, with court appearance scheduled for Monday.

title: Watch: Body cam video shows officer rescuing man, 78, from burning truck
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Easton police officer Dean Soucie and two bystanders rescued a 78-year-old man from a burning truck on Feb. 27.
The accident involved a crash with a disable Mack dump truck on Route 138, leading to a fire in the pickup.
The driver, Francis Leverone, sustained serious injuries but is expected to recover; the crash is still under investigation.
